sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: The upcoming clash between Aston Villa and Fulham is set for May 3, 2025, at 11:30 AM, in Round 35 of the Premier League at Villa Park, Birmingham. Aston Villa, currently sitting in 7th place with 60 points, will host Fulham, who are just behind in 8th with 51 points. This match promises to be a tight encounter given their close league standings. The referee for the day, Robert Jones, averages 3.5 yellow cards per game and 0.12 red cards per game, suggesting a potentially disciplined yet firm approach to officiating.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at their historical encounters, Aston Villa have dominated Fulham in their last 5 matchups, securing 5 wins with no draws or losses. In their most recent meeting on October 19, 2024, Aston Villa triumphed 3-1 away at Fulham’s ground, with Youri Tielemans earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive 8.2 Sofascore rating. Head-to-head trends also show that 7 out of 9 recent matches between these sides have seen over 2.5 goals, indicating a likelihood of a high-scoring game. Additionally, Aston Villa have been the first to score in 4 of their last 5 encounters and have won the first half in 4 of those 5 games.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Aston Villa are coming into this match with a concerning streak of 4 games without a clean sheet, though they’ve been prolific in attack with over 2.5 goals in all of their last 10 matches. Fulham, on the other hand, have struggled defensively, failing to keep a clean sheet in their last 6 games, and have conceded first in 5 of their last 6 matches. Both teams have shown a tendency for goals, with both sides scoring in 5 of Aston Villa’s last 7 games and 4 of Fulham’s last 5. Regarding discipline, both teams have seen less than 4.5 cards in the majority of their recent games, with 9 out of 10 for Villa and 8 out of 10 for Fulham.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Aston Villa
sections.text: Aston Villa boast some standout performers, including Youri Tielemans, who ranks 4th in the league for big chances created with 16 and has an average Sofascore rating of 7.4. Tielemans has also been in form over the last 5 matches, averaging a 7.52 rating with 1 goal and 2 assists. Striker Ollie Watkins leads Villa’s attack with 15 goals against an expected goals (xG) of 14.90, supported by 7 assists. Defender Tyrone Mings is exceptional in ground duels, ranking 1st league-wide with an 88.9% win rate, while Ezri Konsa ranks 3rd with 75.9%. Midfielder John McGinn, ranking 4th in the league for fouls drawn per game at 2.2, will also be crucial in breaking up play.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Fulham
sections.text: For Fulham, Alex Iwobi has been a key figure, ranking high in form over the last 5 matches with a 7.34 Sofascore rating, 2 goals, and 2 assists. Defender Antonee Robinson stands out with 10 assists this season, placing him 3rd in the league for assists from defense. Forward Raúl Jiménez has contributed 10 goals from an xG of 11.19, showing clinical finishing despite a slight underperformance. Midfielder Emile Smith Rowe and winger Harry Wilson have also chipped in with 6 and 5 goals respectively, adding depth to Fulham’s attack.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics and Playing Style
sections.text: Aston Villa have scored 55 goals this season, ranking 9th in the league, and have created 107 big chances, ranking 4th. They excel in ground duels, leading the league with a 52.5% win rate, and rank 4th for headers scored with 10. However, their defense has conceded 49 goals, placing them 12th for least goals conceded, and they’ve only managed 7 clean sheets. Fulham’s goal distribution shows a tendency to score late, with 25.5% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. Both teams average around 6.2 corners per game, with Villa ranking 6th in the league for corners, suggesting set-pieces could play a significant role.

sections.subtitle: Injuries and Absences
sections.text: Aston Villa will miss midfielder Marcus Rashford, who has scored 6 goals and provided 3 assists this season, which could impact their attacking fluidity. Fulham, meanwhile, are without forward Rodrigo Muniz, who has netted 8 goals from an xG of 5.81, a significant loss for their frontline. These absences might force tactical adjustments, potentially affecting the balance of both sides.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts
sections.text: Aston Villa have hit the woodwork 15 times this season, ranking 6th among the unluckiest teams in the league, which could be a factor if their finishing isn’t sharp. Fulham’s defender Joachim Andersen ranks 5th in the league for long balls as a percentage of total passes at 10%, showcasing their direct style at times. Aston Villa’s 76.4% of goals being assisted ranks them 5th in the league, highlighting their team-oriented attacking play. Additionally, 28.6% of Villa’s goals come in the final 15 minutes of games, suggesting they often finish strong.
